root_text.an3.103:1.1: “Pubbeva me, bhikkhave, sambodhā anabhisambuddhassa bodhisattasseva sato etadahosi:
root_text.an3.103:1.2: ‘ko nu kho loke assādo, ko ādīnavo, kiṁ nissaraṇan’ti?
root_text.an3.103:1.4: ‘yaṁ kho lokaṁ paṭicca uppajjati sukhaṁ somanassaṁ, ayaṁ loke assādo.
root_text.an3.103:1.5: Yaṁ loko anicco dukkho vipariṇāmadhammo, ayaṁ loke ādīnavo.
root_text.an3.103:1.6: Yo loke chandarāgavinayo chandarāgappahānaṁ, idaṁ loke nissaraṇan’ti.
root_text.an3.103:1.7: Yāvakīvañcāhaṁ, bhikkhave, evaṁ lokassa assādañca assādato ādīnavañca ādīnavato nissaraṇañca nissaraṇato yathābhūtaṁ nābbhaññāsiṁ, neva tāvāhaṁ, bhikkhave, sadevake loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya ‘anuttaraṁ sammāsambodhiṁ abhisambuddho’ti paccaññāsiṁ.
root_text.an3.103:1.8: Yato ca khvāhaṁ, bhikkhave, evaṁ lokassa assādañca assādato ādīnavañca ādīnavato nissaraṇañca nissaraṇato yathābhūtaṁ abbhaññāsiṁ, athāhaṁ, bhikkhave, sadevake loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya ‘anuttaraṁ sammāsambodhiṁ abhisambuddho’ti paccaññāsiṁ.
root_text.an3.103:1.10: ‘akuppā me vimutti, ayamantimā jāti, natthi dāni punabbhavo’”ti.

translation_text.an3.103:1.1: “Mendicants, before my awakening—when I was still unawakened but intent on awakening—I thought:
translation_text.an3.103:1.2: ‘What’s the gratification in the world? What’s the drawback? What’s the escape?’
translation_text.an3.103:1.4: ‘The pleasure and happiness that arise from the world: this is its gratification.
translation_text.an3.103:1.5: That the world is impermanent, suffering, and perishable: this is its drawback.
translation_text.an3.103:1.6: Removing and giving up desire and greed for the world: this is its escape.’
translation_text.an3.103:1.7: As long as I didn’t truly understand the world’s gratification, drawback, and escape in this way for what they are, I didn’t announce my supreme perfect awakening in this world with its gods, Māras, and Divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its gods and humans.
translation_text.an3.103:1.8: But when I did truly understand the world’s gratification, drawback, and escape in this way for what they are, I announced my supreme perfect awakening in this world with its gods, Māras, and Divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its gods and humans.
translation_text.an3.103:1.9: Knowledge and vision arose in me:
translation_text.an3.103:1.10: ‘My freedom is unshakable; this is my last rebirth; now there’ll be no more future lives.’”

variant_text.an3.103:1.7: abhisambuddho’ti → abhisambuddho (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed, mr)
variant_text.an3.103:1.8: khvāhaṁ → kho ahaṁ (bj, pts1ed); khohaṁ (sya-all, km, mr)
